risks: logistic regression without feature scaling may underperform on digits pixel features
single-run best score may not justify a scientific claim
prediction.rationale: The action runs logistic regression without scaling on the digits dataset, which has 64 numeric pixel features that benefit from scaling. Without scaling, logistic regression is likely to underperform compared to scaled versions or SVC/hist_gradient_boosting. Additionally, a single-run result without robustness verification cannot support strong claims.
prediction.action_specific_signal: logistic regression without scaling on digits typically yields lower accuracy than scaled variants or kernel-based models
prediction.why_this_action_changes_claims: Running unscaled logistic regression provides a baseline but is unlikely to produce a top-performing model or change the final claim, as scaled models or tree ensembles are expected to outperform it.
prediction.why_this_action_may_be_wasteful: It is unlikely to yield a competitive model score that would justify a scientific claim, especially since scaling is known to help logistic regression on this dataset.
prediction.stop_condition: Stop and report if scaled logistic regression or SVC/hist_gradient_boosting achieve significantly higher accuracy with robustness verification.
